title: Hot take: liquidity isn’t the problem anymore - execution is.
author: user_alpha231
selftext:
Onchain perps are fast. Liquidity is deep. UIs are decent.

Yet trading still feels fragmented:

- Two DEXs can look the same and behave completely differently 
- Fills, slippage and delays are inconsistent 
- You end up hardcoding locations instead of trusting routing

I was reading a thread earlier that broke this down well and it really clicked for me:
https://x.com/tide_ag/status/2010667713677631807

It’s starting to feel like execution coordination is the real bottleneck now, not infra speed or raw liquidity.

Is this also your experience, or am I overthinking it?

comments.author: robert_lewis_12
comments.body: 100%. I find myself sticking to specific DEXs not because they have the deepest liquidity or lowest fees, but because I know exactly how their fill engine behaves during volatility.

replies.author: user_alpha231
replies.body: Exactly, that’s what I’m getting at. You end up trusting venues based on how they behave during volatility, not just liquidity or fees. Curious which DEXs you’ve found most consistent in practice?
